reviews.review_msg: Robert is very engaged and kind Prof. He is a funny people to try his best let everyone participate into the class! Chill

Passionate professor with strong academic and industry experience. Very reasonable and approachable. The workload is light (one group paper, one individual paper, one pair presentation, and 10% attendance), but grading can be subjective and the curve is high and tight due to the small class. No clear marking rubrics for assignments were provided.
One of the best profs at beedie!

Rick teaches Bus 484, 482 and 381, if you can take all these classes with this professor, DO SO!  The amount you will learn in his classes will benefit you forever.  Rick is an ivy league professor.  Smart, relaxed, very interested in HR and believes in what he is teaching.  Show interest, attend, & make an effort and you will do well in his class!

Rick is one of the best profs at SFU. He is clearly passionate about HR makes learning really fun and interactive. He has tons of real world experience and shares those stories with the class. Would love to take another class with him. Plus the Aussie accent makes him nice to listen to.
Great professor!  Deeply passionate about H.R. and definitely engages with his entire Seminar!  Would definitely look forward to taking courses with him again!

He is very nice and willing to help. Not so much "hot" as "refined" he dresses well and is probably really rich. He definitely knows how to make HR interesting and fun and he DEFINITELY knows what he's talking about.
Really good guy, enthousiastic, energetic, really gets the students to be interested in the course material.
The best at SFU by far.

He makes you work. Each week there are so many things to read (e.g. articles, lecture notes, powerpoint slides, and case studies which are sometimes 10-15 pages). For a performance management prof, his expectations are not very clear - he marks on what he "feels." Your grade is based on one group project, two individual assignments, & participation
